questions.question: Explain the reflection of light.
questions.answer: Reflection of light is the bouncing back of light when it strikes a smooth surface such as a mirror.

questions.question: State the law of reflection.
questions.answer: The law of reflection states that the angle of incidence is equal to the angle of reflection.

questions.question: What is a plane mirror?
questions.answer: A plane mirror is a flat mirror that forms an upright and virtual image of the same size as the object.

questions.question: Define lateral inversion.
questions.answer: Lateral inversion is the sideways reversal of an image formed in a plane mirror where left appears right and right appears left.

questions.question: Why does a convex mirror provide a wider field of view?
questions.answer: A convex mirror spreads reflected light rays outward, allowing a larger area to be seen.

questions.question: Explain why concave mirrors are used as shaving mirrors.
questions.answer: Concave mirrors form magnified images when the object is placed close to the mirror, making them useful for shaving.

questions.question: What is a concave mirror?
questions.answer: A concave mirror is a mirror that curves inward and can converge light rays to a point.

questions.question: What is a convex mirror?
questions.answer: A convex mirror is a mirror that bulges outward and spreads reflected light rays.

questions.question: What type of image is formed by a plane mirror?
questions.answer: A plane mirror forms a virtual, upright image of the same size as the object.

questions.question: Explain how periscopes use mirrors.
questions.answer: Periscopes use two plane mirrors placed at angles to reflect light and allow viewing objects from hidden positions.

questions.question: What is a lens?
questions.answer: A lens is a transparent curved piece of glass or plastic that refracts light.

questions.question: What is a convex lens?
questions.answer: A convex lens is thicker at the center and converges light rays to a focal point.

questions.question: What is a concave lens?
questions.answer: A concave lens is thinner at the center and spreads light rays outward.

questions.question: Explain how a convex lens forms an image.
questions.answer: A convex lens bends and converges light rays so that they meet at a point to form an image.

questions.question: Why are convex lenses used in magnifying glasses?
questions.answer: Convex lenses converge light rays and form enlarged images when objects are placed close to them.

questions.question: Why are concave lenses used in spectacles for short-sightedness?
questions.answer: Concave lenses spread light rays so that the image forms correctly on the retina.

questions.question: Explain why objects are visible to us.
questions.answer: Objects are visible because they reflect light into our eyes.

questions.question: What is refraction of light?
questions.answer: Refraction is the bending of light when it passes from one medium to another.

questions.question: Why does a straw appear bent in water?
questions.answer: A straw appears bent in water due to refraction of light.

questions.question: Explain how telescopes use lenses.
questions.answer: Telescopes use lenses to collect and focus light from distant objects.

questions.question: Explain how microscopes use lenses.
questions.answer: Microscopes use convex lenses to magnify very small objects.

questions.question: Why do mirrors form images?
questions.answer: Mirrors form images because they reflect light rays coming from objects.

questions.question: Explain why convex mirrors are used as security mirrors in shops.
questions.answer: Convex mirrors allow a wider area to be seen, helping monitor large spaces.

questions.question: What determines the type of image formed by a concave mirror?
questions.answer: The type of image depends on the distance between the object and the mirror.

questions.question: Explain how solar cookers use mirrors.
questions.answer: Solar cookers use concave mirrors to concentrate sunlight at a point to produce heat.
